canonical_definition: Pick-a-Pic is characterized as a large-scale open dataset used to evaluate model outputs against human aesthetic and semantic judgments. The dataset is derived from a web platform that, as one paper notes, collects "real users’ creative intention" ("TIGeR: Unifying Text-to-Image Generation and Retrieval with Large Multimodal Models"). The primary application described for this instrument is to evaluate generation alignment with user preference. In practice, Pick-a-Pic is used to operationalize and measure the construct of Multimodal alignment.
papers: Natural Language Inference Improves Compositionality in Vision-Language Models
PAL: Sample-Efficient Personalized Reward Modeling for Pluralistic Alignment
TIGeR: Unifying Text-to-Image Generation and Retrieval with Large Multimodal Models

definitions.text: A large-scale open dataset derived from a web platform that collects real users' creative intentions, used to evaluate generation alignment with user preference.
definitions.paper: TIGeR: Unifying Text-to-Image Generation and Retrieval with Large Multimodal Models
